executive_summary: Deploy a decision intake layer, triage router, validation gate, and telemetry loop to cut leadership decision latency while preserving rigor, workload realism, and accountability.

main_body.content: Reduce decision latency across product leadership without quality decay while accounting for meeting load and cognitive overhead.
Decision Intake, Triage Router, Review Cadence, Validation Gate, Telemetry Dashboard, and Evidence Registry.
Decision type, urgency, owner, downside risk, reversibility, information completeness, and current workload load.

Classify the request, assign proof threshold, route reversible decisions to a lightweight path, escalate irreversible decisions to the deep-review path, and log outcomes for validation. This causal bridge matters because better input classification improves routing quality and lowers rework.
Any high-downside irreversible decision requires explicit assumptions, owner, review window, and evidence tier.
Decision brief, action owner, review timer, proof-tier tag, and score logs.
Track latency, rework rate, threshold compliance, and post-decision regret rate weekly via telemetry dashboard.
Bypass behavior, ambiguous ownership, unsupported causal claims, and hidden workload cost can break the model.

Stabilize intake -> deploy routing -> pilot one team -> validate metrics -> optimize cadence -> scale. Account for fatigue, meeting density, and attentional fragmentation before adding new review burden.

action_items: Pilot with one product team for two weeks
Instrument a weekly latency and rework dashboard
